summary: Alfred has solid foundational documentation with clear architecture explanation and comprehensive feature list. Primary gaps exist in high-complexity module documentation (app.rs, bridge.rs, editor_state.rs contain 65% of code but lack detailed inline docs). Strong README and development guidelines; weak changelog discipline.

complexity_doc_gap.issue: Module doc present but handle_key_event (605 lines) lacks internal comments explaining control flow branches
Good overview doc; each register_* function has minimal comment; primitives not cross-referenced
Type docs present; 39 public fields documented as group but lack per-field guidance on mutation patterns

stale_comments.comment_snippet: // :g/TODO/d on 5-line buffer with 2 TODOs -> 3 lines remain

sections: Editing commands (17 commands documented with examples)
Plugins (built-in list, example plugin, creating plugins)
readme_assessment.notes: Well-written and comprehensive for user-facing features. Installation uses Make; setup tests via `make test` and `make e2e`. Good organization with tables and code examples.

adr_assessment.note: No Architecture Decision Records found. Project decisions documented informally in code comments and REFACTORING_SUMMARY.md.

changelog_assessment.note: No CHANGELOG.md file. Relying on git commit messages. Recent commits follow conventional format (feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:, chore:, test:) with good discipline (6+ commits in last 3 days). Latest: 'clean(alfred-core): remove unused facade functions' (2026-03-25).

topics: Commit strategy (small steps, all tests must pass)
Testing requirements (unit tests, E2E tests, mock tautology, Farley Index)
rust_lisp constraints (empty strings, boolean handling, equality, scoping)
Architecture principles (functional-core/imperative-shell, plugin-first, zero core/plugin coupling)
Quality gates (fmt, clippy, tests, E2E, mock tautology, Farley Index)

content: Project structure (6-crate Cargo workspace, plugin-first, Lisp interpreter, TUI stack, text buffer)

test_targets: make e2e (end-to-end with pexpect and Docker)
make ci-local (run full CI via act)

recommendations.title: Document mega-file internal structure
recommendations.description: Add section-dividers and internal documentation to app.rs (6882 lines) and bridge.rs (5669 lines). Break long methods into smaller functions with clear intent and document control flow branches in handle_key_event (605 lines).

recommendations.title: Create CHANGELOG.md
recommendations.description: Extract last 20 conventional commits into CHANGELOG.md with categories (Features, Fixes, Refactoring, Documentation). Update with each release.

recommendations.title: Add Lisp primitive cross-reference
recommendations.description: Create docs/LISP_PRIMITIVES.md documenting all 40+ registered Lisp functions with signatures, examples, and which plugins use them. Currently only in README bullet list.

recommendations.title: Plugin development guide
recommendations.description: Expand README plugin section into docs/PLUGIN_DEVELOPMENT.md with lifecycle, hook API, command registration patterns, and 2-3 advanced examples.

recommendations.title: Document facade module usage
recommendations.description: Add doc comment to facade.rs explaining the abstraction boundary pattern and why callers should use facade:: instead of direct submodule access. Link from main crate docs.
